verdict_reasoning: ABCB9/TAPL is a multi-pass ABC transporter that resides exclusively on lysosomal membranes. Its function is to translocate cytosolic peptides into the lysosomal lumen, powered by ATP hydrolysis. The TMD0 N-terminal domain encodes a lysosomal targeting signal; full-length TAPL traffics directly from ER→Golgi→lysosomes and colocalizes essentially 100% with LAMP markers. The nucleotide-binding domains face the cytosol. No extracellular-facing loops are exposed to the cell exterior. Contextual buckets: (1) cell_state_induced — no evidence of stress- or activation-induced PM display for full-length TAPL; (2) tissue_restricted_surface — not applicable, localization is lysosomal not PM in any lineage; (3) lysosomal_exocytosis — no published evidence of TAPL reaching the plasma membrane via lysosomal exocytosis; (4) dual_localization — only a truncated core construct lacking TMD0 mislocalizes partly to the PM; full-length wt-TAPL traffics exclusively to lysosomes; (5) stable_surface_attachment — TAPL is not secreted and not known to attach to any PM partner extracellularly. No antibody/ADC/CAR-T therapeutic programs targeting TAPL on the cell surface are documented. Verdict: lysosomal membrane resident, no extracellular face accessible.
user_message:
Triage the human gene **ABCB9**.

Canonical identifiers and gene summary (machine-resolved from HGNC and NCBI; no further lookups available — judge from the context below plus your trained knowledge):

- HGNC approved name: ATP binding cassette subfamily B member 9
- HGNC symbol: ABCB9
- UniProt accession: Q9NP78
- Aliases: EST122234, TAPL
- Previous symbols: (none)
- HGNC gene-group memberships: ATP binding cassette subfamily B
- CD nomenclature: (none assigned)
- NCBI summary: The membrane-associated protein encoded by this gene is a member of the superfamily of ATP-binding cassette (ABC) transporters. ABC proteins transport various molecules across extra- and intra-cellular membranes. ABC genes are divided into seven distinct subfamilies (ABC1, MDR/TAP, MRP, ALD, OABP, GCN20, White). This protein is a member of the MDR/TAP subfamily. Members of the MDR/TAP subfamily are involved in multidrug resistance as well as antigen presentation. This family member functions in the translocation of peptides from the cytosol into the lysosomal lumen. Alternative splicing of th…

Emit one JSON object matching the `TriageRecordDraft` schema as your **entire** response — no prose around it, no markdown code fences, no commentary. Required keys: `verdict`, `verdict_reasoning`, `reason`.
